issues.reason: Monster annotations assign special 15 to (7,7) and special 18 to (11,7), but the concrete floor map only places enemy codes 203 and 209, whose current stats both have special=0. There is no floor data, event data, or static special source showing these as actual zone/repulse monsters.
issues.required_change: Make the intended specials concrete in the floor data using whitelist specials with at most one special per monster, or remove the special-pressure claims and rebalance/compensate the placements as ordinary no-special enemies.

required_changes: Make the intended specials concrete in the floor data using whitelist specials with at most one special per monster, or remove the special-pressure claims and rebalance/compensate the placements as ordinary no-special enemies.
summary: Local monster review passed. | Monster codes are allowed and enemy tiles are not orthogonally adjacent, but the claimed special placements are not concrete in the actual floor data.
